chief_complaint: An 82-year-old female presented with a 2-year history of chronic cough and an acute exacerbation.
history: The patient had a 2-year history of chronic cough and an acute exacerbation. The patient's symptoms worsened over time.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Initial diagnosis was severe pneumonia. Definitive diagnosis of lipoid pneumonia was confirmed by computed tomography-guided lung biopsy, which revealed lipid-laden macrophages.
